title: Promoting Engagement Through the Optimization of Digital Just-in-Time Adaptive Health Interventions
authors_full: Lessons Learned Paper (in preparation): Stephanie M. Carpenter, Stuart Case, Elizabeth Gerwe, Mia Liza A. Lustria, Laura Reid Marks, Karen MacDonell, Sylvie Naar, & Yan Wang
authors_short: Lessons Learned Paper  Stephanie M. Carpenter Stuart Case Elizabeth Gerwe Mia Liza A. Lustria Laura Reid Marks Karen MacDonell Sylvie Naar & Yan Wang

summary.hook: This poster describes a micro-randomized trial (MRT) feasibility study aimed at optimizing a just-in-time adaptive intervention (JITAI) to increase wear time of a wrist-worn...
summary.summary_30s: This poster describes a micro-randomized trial (MRT) feasibility study aimed at optimizing a just-in-time adaptive intervention (JITAI) to increase wear time of a wrist-worn alcohol biosensor (Skyn) among young adults with HIV.

general: What is the main takeaway you hope attendees remember from this poster?
What surprised you most once you started digging into this topic?
Why does this work matter beyond this specific poster session?
undergrad: What part of this project would be most helpful for an undergraduate student to learn from?
What skills or tools were most important for getting this project done?
If an undergraduate wanted to get involved in work like this, where would you suggest they start?
grad: How were participants assigned to Standard (69%) vs Engagement (31%)—randomization, availability, or staged rollout?
What was the key design or analysis tradeoff in this project?
If you were extending this into a next study, what would you tighten or measure differently?
researcher: How were participants assigned to Standard (69%) vs Engagement (31%)—randomization, availability, or staged rollout?
What exactly counts as “wear time” in the last 12 hours (minutes threshold, handling of charging/off-wrist time)?
What were the two daily microrandomization time points, and what contextual data (EMA/sensor) were used to decide eligibility at each point?
clinician: How could these findings affect clinical care, screening, or decision-making in practice?
What kind of patient or care setting would this poster be most relevant for?
What would need to happen before this work would be ready for broader clinical use?
community: Who in the community is most likely to benefit if this work moves forward?
What kinds of community partnerships would strengthen the next phase of this project?
What would meaningful real-world impact look like for the people touched by this work?
leader: What type of support, infrastructure, or investment would help this work move further?
What is the clearest implementation or scale-up opportunity that comes out of this poster?
If a leader wanted to back the next step, what outcome would be most important to track?
